title: One Person Company Registration
mainHeading: One Person Company (OPC) Registration in India

hero.headline: Start Your OPC with Simplicity and Legal Safety
hero.subtext: Launch your one-person business with the benefits of limited liability and legal recognition under the Companies Act, 2013 — with Professionals Law’s professional support.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: A One Person Company (OPC) is a form of private limited company introduced under the Companies Act, 2013, designed specifically for those entrepreneurs who want to run the show independently.
It allows a single individual to incorporate and run a company with limited liability and full control, combining the benefits of a sole proprietorship and a corporate entity.

OPCs must comply with certain statutory obligations such as maintaining books of accounts, filing annual returns and auditing (if applicable), but they enjoy lighter compliance compared to other corporate structures.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: OPC is ideal for individual entrepreneurs who want to start a business in their name but also want to benefit from corporate structure, limited liability and formal recognition.
It provides a professional image and makes it easier to access bank loans, contracts and legal protections, without requiring a partner or co-founder.

body.title: Limited Liability
body.description: The personal assets of the owner are protected and the liability is limited to the amount invested in the business.

body.title: Separate Legal Entity
body.description: OPC enjoys its own legal identity, separate from its sole member.

body.title: Ease of Management
body.description: Single person control allows quicker decision-making and flexibility in operations.

body.title: Lesser Compliance
body.description: Compared to other company types, OPC has relaxed compliance in terms of meetings and filing.

criteria: Only one individual who is an Indian citizen and resident in India (staying in India for at least 120 days during the financial year) can form an OPC.
The individual must appoint a nominee who will take over the company in case of death or incapacity.
An individual cannot incorporate more than one OPC or be a nominee in more than one OPC.
OPC cannot carry out Non-Banking Financial Investment or investment activities.

documents: Address proof (Bank Statement, Electricity Bill, etc.)

body.description: Obtain DSC for the proposed director.
Reserve Company Name using the SPICe+ Part A form.
Submit SPICe+ Part B along with MOA, AOA, AGILE-PRO, Form INC-3 and other required documents.
PAN & TAN applications are included in the incorporation process.
Receive Certificate of Incorporation from the Registrar of Companies.
The registration of a One Person Company typically takes 10 to 15 working days, depending on document verification and MCA approvals.

body.question: Can a foreign national form an OPC in India?
body.answer: No, only Indian citizens who are residents in India can form an OPC.

body.question: Is it mandatory to appoint a nominee in OPC?
body.answer: Yes, a nominee must be appointed while incorporating an OPC to take over in case of the owner’s death or incapacity.

body.question: Can I convert my OPC into a Private Limited Company?
body.answer: Yes, OPCs can be voluntarily converted into Private Limited Companies after 2 years or mandatorily if their turnover exceeds ₹2 crore or paid-up capital exceeds ₹50 lakh.

body.question: Do OPCs need to hold annual general meetings (AGM)?
body.answer: No, AGMs are not required for OPCs.

body.question: Can OPC have more than one director?
body.answer: Yes, while only one person can be the member/shareholder, there can be more than one director, but not more than 15.

body.question: What taxes are applicable to OPCs?
body.answer: OPCs are taxed as private companies and are eligible for deductions under Startup India (if applicable).

body.question: Is audit mandatory for OPC?
body.answer: Yes, if the turnover exceeds ₹2 crore or paid-up capital exceeds ₹50 lakh, audit is mandatory.

body.question: What is the capital requirement for starting an OPC?
body.answer: There is no minimum capital requirement for starting an OPC.

body.question: Can an OPC raise funding from investors?
body.answer: OPCs cannot raise equity funding like private or public limited companies but can raise loans or convert into a Private Limited Company for funding.
